‚óê The user hasn't sent a message yet - this is just the system setting up the 
  terminal identity. The system message says "no response needed." I should wait
   for the user to send an actual message.

  Wait, let me re-read. The user message contains the system notification about
  terminal identity being set. It says "this is a configuration step, no 
  response needed." So I should just acknowledge briefly and wait for the user's
   actual request.

  Actually, looking more carefully, there's no actual user prompt here beyond 
  the system setup. I should just briefly acknowledge I'm ready.

‚óè Ready. What can I help with?
